headline: Delta Air Lines Tests Event Driven Trans Atlantic Capacity With Football Flights
summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) is adding special trans Atlantic flights tied to major football events. The company is increasing capacity on select routes to handle event driven international travel demand. These flights are positioned as temporary additions rather than routine schedule adjustments. For investors watching NYSE:DAL, this move sits alongside a share price of $71.55 and a 1 year return of 44.0%. Over 3 years the stock has returned 106.0%, and over 5 years 62.8%, which gives useful...

headline: Berkshire adds Delta and Macy’s stakes in first quarter
summary: Investing.com -- Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRKa) disclosed new positions in Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) and Macy’s (NYSE:M) during the first quarter under Chief Executive Greg Abel, according to a regulatory filing Friday afternoon. Shares rallied 3% and 5%, respectively, after hours on Friday amid the news.

Shares rallied 3% and 5%, respectively, after hours on Friday amid the news.

headline: Berkshire Hathaway triples Alphabet stake and invests in Delta and Macy's under new CEO
summary: Berkshire Hathaway more than tripled the size of its investment in Google's parent company and bought over $2.6 billion worth of Delta Airlines stock as Greg Abel settled into the CEO job after taking over from Warren Buffett at the start of the year.  The conglomerate also dumped a number of other stocks, including Visa, Mastercard, Domino's Pizza, Amazon and United Healthcare after the departure late last year of Todd Combs, who was one of the two investment mangers Buffett hired to help manage the portfolio.  Buffett was always reluctant to invest in tech companies because he said he didn't understand them well enough to predict the long-term winners.

content.title: Berkshire Hathaway triples Alphabet stake and invests in Delta and Macy's under new CEO
content.summary: Berkshire Hathaway more than tripled the size of its investment in Google's parent company and bought over $2.6 billion worth of Delta Airlines stock as Greg Abel settled into the CEO job after taking over from Warren Buffett at the start of the year.  The conglomerate also dumped a number of other stocks, including Visa, Mastercard, Domino's Pizza, Amazon and United Healthcare after the departure late last year of Todd Combs, who was one of the two investment mangers Buffett hired to help manage the portfolio.  Buffett was always reluctant to invest in tech companies because he said he didn't understand them well enough to predict the long-term winners.

thumbnail.caption: People walk past large portraits of Berkshire Hathaway's Warren Buffett, left, and CEO Greg Abel inside the Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting on Saturday, May 2, 2026, in Omaha, Neb. (AP Photo/Rebecca S. Gratz)
